howItWorks.intro: This marine battery calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Total Ah Needed = Daily Use × Days at Anchor
Bank Size = Total Ah ÷ Max Depth of Discharge
Lead acid: 50% DoD max, LiFePO4: 80% DoD max
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How do I calculate daily amp-hour usage?
faq.answer: Add up each device's amps × hours of use. Example: LED lights (2A × 5hr = 10Ah) + fridge (5A × 24hr = 120Ah) + instruments (1A × 12hr = 12Ah). Use a clamp meter for accurate readings.

faq.question: AGM vs LiFePO4 for boats?
faq.answer: LiFePO4 costs 2-3× more upfront but lasts 5-10× longer, weighs 60% less, charges faster, and provides 80% usable capacity vs 50% for AGM. For frequent cruising, LiFePO4 has lower lifetime cost.

faq.question: What depth of discharge is safe?
faq.answer: Flooded and AGM lead-acid: 50% maximum to preserve lifespan. LiFePO4: 80% regularly, 100% occasionally. Discharging lead-acid below 50% dramatically reduces cycle life.

faq.question: Is the Marine Battery Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard boating and marine formulas trusted by boaters and sailors. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Marine Battery Calculator?
sections.body: The Marine Battery Calculator is a free online tool designed for boaters and sailors who need quick, accurate calculations in the boating and marine space. By entering your daily use, days at anchor, battery type, you get instant results including total ah needed, battery bank size, batteries needed. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting total ah needed right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In boating and marine, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by boaters and sailors worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to navigate safely with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your daily use and need to find the right total ah needed. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.
